methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Matic Robotics is a seed-stage consumer tech company founded by Navneet Dalal and Mehul Nariyawala that has raised $107M to date. The comparable universe for this analysis is limited, comprising one identified private peer (Vital Lyfe) and no relevant public comparables in the consumer tech robotics space.

Stage-progression analysis, the applicable methodology given available data, uses a $6M base valuation derived from total capital raised and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement. This produces an implied valuation of $16M. The triangulated analysis across available methods yields a valuation range of $14M (low) to $19M (median $16M), reflecting conservative and expansive interpretations of the stage-progression framework.

Key uncertainties materially constrain confidence in this range. The single-method reliance reflects sparse comparable data and limits cross-validation of results. The substantial gap between total capital raised ($107M) and the derived valuation ($14M–$19M) suggests either prior down rounds, substantial dilution through option pools, or potential data inconsistencies requiring clarification. The consumer tech robotics sector lacks sufficient public or private benchmark depth for revenue multiple or venture-capital-return-expectation triangulation. Validation of this range depends critically on obtaining additional peer transaction data and clarifying the composition and timing of the $107M raise.
